nodes.label: Slider Publish Date + Quick Toggle Design
nodes.norm_label: slider publish date + quick toggle design

nodes.label: Ok & Mürekkep Visual Identity
nodes.rationale: Old navy+gold identity abandoned for a fresh warm-neutral background with vermilion (target-red) accent, large editorial typography and minimalism; overline labels replace the old gold underline.

nodes.label: Two-App Isolation Architecture
nodes.rationale: Public (Astro SSR, read-only, no public API) and admin (Vite React SPA + Netlify Functions, schema owner) deploy as two separate Netlify sites sharing only the Neon DB, so a breach of the public site cannot directly reach the admin panel.

nodes.label: Admin Authentication & Security Model
nodes.rationale: Single shared bcrypt-hashed password (no account system, Google OAuth removed) with HS256 JWT in an httpOnly+Secure+SameSite=Strict cookie (never localStorage, to block XSS token theft); bcryptjs chosen over bcrypt to avoid native bindings on Netlify Functions; Origin header check guards mutations.

nodes.label: Database Schema (sliders, announcements, login_attempts)
nodes.rationale: admin_users and settings tables intentionally omitted because the tournament link was severed and a single-password model is used; public visibility rule is is_active = TRUE AND publish_date <= NOW().

nodes.label: Admin API Path-Based Router Endpoints
nodes.rationale: Single Netlify Function with a path-based router; /api/sliders/reorder must be registered before /api/sliders/:id so the static route wins.

nodes.label: Slider Publish/End-Date Window
nodes.rationale: Sliders gain publish_date (default NOW()) and optional end_date (NULL = indefinite) to match announcements' scheduling; public query filters is_active AND publish_date <= NOW() AND (end_date IS NULL OR end_date >= NOW()); end_date < publish_date rejected with 400.

nodes.label: Quick Active/Passive Toggle
nodes.rationale: Status badge becomes a clickable button that re-sends the full object with is_active inverted to the existing PUT endpoint, so no new backend route is needed since list queries already return the full object.

nodes.label: Rate Limit Module (rateLimit.ts, Sql type)
nodes.rationale: IP_LIMIT=5, GLOBAL_LIMIT=20, WINDOW_MINUTES=15; DB-backed via login_attempts table; IP source prefers x-nf-client-connection-ip (Netlify) then first x-forwarded-for value.

nodes.label: Auth Handlers (auth.ts: login, logout, me)
nodes.rationale: Rate-limit check runs before bcrypt so a 429 short-circuits before any password work; bcryptjs used to avoid native bindings.
